{"id":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","icon":null,"color":"#bec2c8","name":"Hey Chill","summary":"Voice-first AI companion — dogfooding OpenClaw","description":"## Final Goal

Kim uses it daily for hands-free interaction with their agents.

## Approach (revised 2026-03-05)

Standalone PWA — decoupled from AIRI. Installable on phone/tablet/desktop.

* Always-on wake word (Hey Chill) OR push-to-talk button
* Voice → transcription → send to Chill via Slack (n8n webhook)
* No Live2D, no AIRI complexity — just a mic and a button
* AIRI repo preserved for later if needed

## Live URL

[https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app](<https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app>) (deployed as Brainwave)

## Repo

chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill

---

## Features (cumulative — updated after each milestone)

### Push-to-Talk

* Press and hold button to record voice
* Uses Web Speech API for real-time transcription
* Visual feedback during recording

### Voice Activity Detection (VAD)

* Auto-stops recording after 3.5 seconds of silence
* Allows natural pauses between sentences without cutting off

### Transcription

* Real-time speech-to-text via Web Speech API
* Interim results shown during recording
* Final transcript captured on recording end

### Slack Integration

* Sends transcription to Chill agent via n8n webhook
* Webhook URL: /webhook/hey-chill
* Message delivered to Slack channel

### PWA

* Installable as progressive web app
* Service worker for offline shell
* Works on mobile and desktop browsers","url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/project/hey-chill-335516e3da6b","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:51:01.776Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:21:25.560Z","startDate":"2026-03-01","targetDate":null,"labels":[],"initiatives":[],"lead":{},"status":{"id":"45fae361-8f51-4684-a479-68682e997bd2","name":"In Progress"},"teams":[{"id":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","name":"Forge","key":"FRG"}],"resources":[{"type":"link","id":"54361443-e0ef-4000-a58c-1802bff05f45","label":"Brainwave (LAN)","url":"https://192.168.101.151:3005","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:07.760Z"},{"type":"link","id":"92fbc355-5dce-4fca-b9ee-d21de9658b19","label":"GitHub","url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:07.564Z"},{"type":"link","id":"c515dc96-8458-4df6-b82e-ed59939657a3","label":"Hey Chill PWA","url":"https://hey-chill.vercel.app","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:07.376Z"}]}

Now I have a good understanding of the Hey Chill app. Let me analyze what I found:

**App Structure:**
- Title: "Hey Chill"
- Mic button with push-to-talk functionality
- Web Speech API for transcription
- VAD silence timeout: 3500ms (3.5s)
- Slack/n8n webhook via localStorage setting
- Settings modal for webhook URL
- Message history display
- PWA: manifest.json + service worker (sw.js)

**DOM Elements:**
- `#mic-btn` - the microphone button
- `#mic-ring` - animation ring


- `#mic-hint` - status text that toggles between "Tap to speak" and "Tap to stop"
- `#live-transcript` and `#live-text` - display the real-time transcription as the user speaks
- `#transcript-bar` and `#transcript-text` - show the finalized transcript once recording stops
- `#send-btn` and `#discard-btn` - action buttons to submit or clear the message
- `#messages` and `#messages-empty` - the conversation history and its empty state
- `#not-supported` - fallback message if Web Speech API isn't available
- `#settings-btn` and `#modal-overlay` - the settings gear icon and the modal for configuring the webhook URL
- `#modal-close`, `#webhook-url`, and `#save-settings` - controls within the settings modal

The core behavior involves tapping the mic to toggle recording, with a 3.5-second silence threshold that auto-stops the recording, then POSTing the transcribed text to a webhook endpoint along with metadata. Messages track their delivery state through sending, sent, error, or no-webhook states. The app also registers as a PWA with a manifest and service worker, so I need to verify those files exist and are properly configured. content.thinking:
One test failed: `VAD — new speech result resets the silence timer`. 

The issue is that `addInitScript` runs in document creation, but the script I used inside the test tries to use two `addInitScript` calls - one in `beforeEach` and one in the test itself. The `addInitScript` in the test body runs before page load, but the `page.goto` already happened in `beforeEach` - so the second `addInitScript` in the test doesn't apply to the already-loaded page.

I need to fix this test. The approach of using `addInitScript` inside the test body (after page.goto has already been called in beforeEach) won't work because `addInitScript` only affects future navigations.


I should use `page.evaluate` to inject the spy after the page has already loaded, rather than trying to add another init script that won't take effect on the current page.

# hey-chill-qa

Playwright regression suite for the [Hey Chill](https://hey-chill.vercel.app) PWA.

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
cp .env.example .env   # edit BASE_URL if needed
```

## Running tests

| Command | What runs |
|---|---|
| `npm test` | All suites (smoke → regression → pwa) |
| `npm run test:smoke` | Smoke tests only |
| `npm run test:regression` | Regression tests only |
| `npm run test:pwa` | PWA / service-worker tests only |
| `npm run test:report` | Open last HTML report |

## Test structure

```
tests/
├── smoke/
│   └── smoke.spec.js          # Basic load & DOM element checks (10 tests)
├── regression/
│   ├── push-to-talk.spec.js   # PTT button states & CSS classes (9 tests)
│   ├── vad.spec.js            # 3.5 s silence auto-stop (3 tests)
│   ├── transcription.spec.js  # Live + final transcript display (8 tests)
│   ├── webhook.spec.js        # Fetch intercept, payload, statuses (7 tests)
│   └── settings.spec.js       # Modal open/close, save, ARIA (10 tests)
└── pwa/
    └── pwa.spec.js            # Manifest, service worker, HTTPS (16 tests)
```

**Total: 65 tests**

## Configuration

`BASE_URL` is read from `.env` (defaults to `https://hey-chill.vercel.app`).

```ini
BASE_URL=https://hey-chill.vercel.app
```

## Notes on Web Speech API

The Web Speech API (`SpeechRecognition`) is not available in headless Chromium.
All tests that exercise voice-related UI inject a `MockSpeechRecognition` via
`page.addInitScript`, replacing `window.SpeechRecognition` before `app.js`
runs. This lets us simulate `onstart`, `onresult`, and `onend` events and
verify UI state transitions without real audio.

Webhook tests use Playwright's `page.route()` to intercept `fetch` calls so
no real n8n/Slack endpoint is needed.
